content.full_text:
July 21, 1976

Honorable Allen T. Lynch, (Ret.)
The Superior Court
State of California
Post Office Box 1605
Palm Springs, California  92262

Dear Judge Lynch:

     This letter is in response to your recent inquiry concerning the financial disclosure requirements under the Political Reform Act for retired judges receiving assignments from the court.

     We are currently drafting a definition of "judges of courts of record" for the purpose of determining who have filing obligations under the law.  The Commission will review a staff recommendation at its August meeting and I will let you know the decision of the Commission as soon as it is available.

                                            Very truly yours,


                                            Regina Siciliano-Kutchins
                                            Technical Assistance Program
                                            Coordinator

sections.question_synthetic: Whether retired judges receiving court assignments are subject to financial disclosure requirements under the Political Reform Act, specifically regarding the definition of 'judges of courts of record.'
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC is currently drafting a definition of 'judges of courts of record' to determine filing obligations under the law. The Commission will review a staff recommendation at its August meeting and provide a final decision to the inquirer.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Clear primary document with legible content. Secondary pink document is too degraded for reliable extraction and appears to be a different (possibly 1978) letter with topic related to juvenile law rather than Political Reform Act. Only primary 1976 letter data extracted. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present; letter is informal guidance response indicating decision pending.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether retired judges receiving court assignments are subject to financial disclosure requirements under the Political Reform Act, specifically regarding the definition of 'judges of courts of record.'

CONCLUSION: The FPPC is currently drafting a definition of 'judges of courts of record' to determine filing obligations under the law. The Commission will review a staff recommendation at its August meeting and provide a final decision to the inquirer.

We are currently drafting a definition of "judges of courts of record" for the purpose of determining who have filing obligations under the law. embedding.summary: FPPC response to Honorable Allen T. Lynch (Ret.) regarding financial disclosure requirements for retired judges with court assignments under the Political Reform Act. The Commission is developing a definition of covered judges and will provide guidance following its August meeting review.
